BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Monthly household surplus check against the baseline expense outline. Take-home is $3,800 (semimonthly $3,812.44 x2 avg, minus intra-month fluctuation). Baseline expenses total $2,630, leaving a $1,170 surplus toward savings, unexpected medical costs, and home repairs.

OtherIncome.Note: SSI and interest are ring-fenced (SSI to Marcus custodial, interest reinvested); household surplus math uses RN take-home only.

CurrentState.SurplusDestination: Savings, unexpected medical costs, home repairs
CurrentState.Note: Personal household break-even. John Martin (widower, RN at Rutherford Family Medicine). Monthly take-home baseline $3,800 (persona/MEMORY.md:20); baseline expenses $2,630 (persona/MEMORY.md:21); baseline surplus $1,170 routed to Pinnacle savings + Marcus SSI custodial. Current-cycle actual medical line $116.13 vs $95 baseline (see obsidian household/reconciliation/monthly-baseline.md).

Scenario_A_MedicalUp.Label: Medical line drifts $30/mo (biologic add or supply creep)
Scenario_A_MedicalUp.Note: Still comfortably above $0. Bigger biologic co-share would need HSA / BCBS-TN routing.

Scenario_B_UtilitiesWinter.Label: Winter utilities creep as heating season starts

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Note: N/A - homeowner with mortgage, no rent scenario. See Scenario_A_MedicalUp for the persona-appropriate stress test.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: N/A - homeowner household (Studio Scenario A: rent to $750). Persona-appropriate stress test in Scenario_A_MedicalUp.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: N/A - homeowner with mortgage. Persona break-even is calculated in Scenario_A_MedicalUp (medical drift) and Scenario_B_UtilitiesWinter (seasonal utilities).
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Not applicable. John is a homeowner and has no membership-based revenue; the Studio rent-increase scenario does not apply to this household.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Note: N/A - homeowner with mortgage, no rent scenario. See Scenario_B_UtilitiesWinter for the persona-appropriate stress test.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: N/A - homeowner household (Studio Scenario B: rent to $850). Persona-appropriate stress test in Scenario_B_UtilitiesWinter.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.BreakEvenCalculation: N/A - homeowner with mortgage. Persona break-even is calculated in Scenario_A_MedicalUp (medical drift) and Scenario_B_UtilitiesWinter (seasonal utilities).
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Not applicable. John is a homeowner and has no membership-based revenue; the Studio rent-increase scenario does not apply to this household.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Baseline surplus of $1,170 comfortably absorbs a $30-50 monthly drift on medical or utilities. Any single-cycle drift above $150 total is a flag for the quarterly review.
ActionItems: Route reconciliation to Airtable household base each cycle
